seo_description: Current local time in Chicago, Illinois. CST (UTC-6) in winter, CDT (UTC-5) in summer. Live clock, CME trading hours, and Chicago time difference to New York, London, LA.

faq.question: What time is it in Chicago right now?
faq.answer: Chicago operates on Central Standard Time (CST/UTC-6) from early November to mid-March, and Central Daylight Time (CDT/UTC-5) from mid-March to early November. Chicago is 1 hour behind New York and 2 hours ahead of Los Angeles. The live clock above shows the current Chicago time updated in real time.

faq.question: What time zone is Chicago in?
faq.answer: Chicago is in the Central Time Zone, using Central Standard Time (CST/UTC-6) in winter and Central Daylight Time (CDT/UTC-5) in summer. The Central Time Zone covers a large swath of the US including Illinois, Texas, Minnesota, Missouri, and Louisiana. Chicago is the largest city in the Central Time Zone and a major US business hub.

faq.question: Does Chicago observe daylight saving time?
faq.answer: Yes. Chicago clocks spring forward 1 hour to Central Daylight Time (CDT/UTC-5) on the second Sunday of March, and fall back to Central Standard Time (CST/UTC-6) on the first Sunday of November. This is the same schedule as the rest of the contiguous US (except Arizona and Hawaii). The change affects Chicago's offset from London by 1 hour: 6 hours behind in winter, 5 hours behind in summer.

faq.question: What is the time difference between Chicago and New York?
faq.answer: Chicago is consistently 1 hour behind New York year-round. Both cities observe daylight saving time on the same schedule, maintaining a constant 1-hour gap regardless of season. When it is 9:00 AM in New York, it is 8:00 AM in Chicago. When it is 5:00 PM in Chicago, it is 6:00 PM in New York.

faq.question: What is the time difference between Chicago and Los Angeles?
faq.answer: Chicago is consistently 2 hours ahead of Los Angeles year-round. Both cities observe daylight saving time on the same schedule, so the gap is always exactly 2 hours. When it is 9:00 AM in Los Angeles, it is 11:00 AM in Chicago. This 2-hour difference is a constant factor for US west–midwest business scheduling.

faq.question: What is the time difference between Chicago and London?
faq.answer: Chicago is 6 hours behind London during Central Standard Time (November–March) and 5 hours behind during Central Daylight Time (CDT) when the UK is on BST (March–October). When it is 9:00 AM in Chicago (CST), it is 3:00 PM in London. The best window for Chicago–London calls is 9:00 AM–11:00 AM Chicago time (3:00–5:00 PM London), before the UK business day closes.

faq.question: What time does the Chicago Mercantile Exchange open?
faq.answer: The Chicago Mercantile Exchange (CME) operates nearly 24 hours for electronic trading (Sunday–Friday 5:00 PM–4:00 PM CT, with a 60-minute daily halt). The CME's main open outcry trading pits historically ran 8:30 AM–3:15 PM CT. Chicago is also home to the Chicago Board of Trade (CBOT) and Chicago Board Options Exchange (CBOE), making it the world's largest derivatives trading centre.

faq.question: What is the best time to call Chicago from Europe?
faq.answer: From London (GMT in winter), the best time to call Chicago is between 3:00 PM and 6:00 PM GMT (9:00 AM–12:00 PM CST). During UK summer (BST/UTC+1), call between 2:00 PM and 5:00 PM BST to reach Chicago in the morning. From Paris or Berlin (CET/UTC+1 in winter), call between 4:00 PM and 7:00 PM local time to catch Chicago's morning business hours.
